data.title: Patrick Industries closes on deal for Medallion Instrumentation Systems
data.description:
Patrick Industries (NASDAQ:PATK) announced on Monday that it closed on its acquisition of Medallion Instrumentation Systems on Monday. The deal price for the premier provider of customized instrumentation and vehicle electronics for marine, RV, powersports, on-highway and military markets was undisclosed.

Medallion's engineering team was highlighted as providing complete systems solutions that cater to customer needs, including digital switching, lighting controls, integrated audio, sensor products, wire harnessing, gauges and LCD touchscreen displays. Medallion's 2024 revenue was approximately $38 million.

"We are thrilled to welcome the entire Medallion team to the Patrick family as we advance our mission of providing full-solutions packages to our customers," said Andy Nemeth, Chief Executive Officer of Patrick.

"With the growing customer preference towards digital switching, custom LCD displays, gauges and controllers in the Outdoor Enthusiast space, this acquisition purposefully connects our complement of existing product offerings into a complete technology solution that can be customized to our customer's vision and needs," noted Patrick Industries (NASDAQ:PATK) CEO Andy Nemeth. "In tandem with our strong mechanical expertise and customer relationships, Medallion's engineering, design, and sales capabilities offer an exciting opportunity to further expand our presence in the marine, powersports, and RV markets." he added.

Following its absorption by Patrick Industries (PATK), Medallion will continue to operate under its existing brand name and within its current facility.

Shares of Patrick Industries (PATK) are up 8.6% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Strategy buys 20.4K bitcoin last week
data.description:
• Strategy (NASDAQ:MSTR) resumed its bitcoin purchases and acquired 20.4K bitcoin (BTC-USD) in the week ended February 23, the company said in a filing.
  • The firm said it paid ~$1.99B in cash at an average price of ~$97,514 per bitcoin in the period between February 18 and February 23.
  • The purchases were made using proceeds from the convertible notes offering.
  • As of February 23, the firm, along with its units, held an aggregate of ~499,096 bitcoins, which were acquired at an aggregate purchase price of ~$33.1 billion.
  • Strategy (NASDAQ:MSTR) had not acquired any bitcoin (BTC-USD) in the period between Feb. 10 and 17.

data.title: Carebook announces successful closing of take-private transaction with UIL
data.description:
• Carebook Technologies (CRBKF:CA) has announced the closing of the previously announced plan of arrangement.
  • Pursuant to which UIL has acquired all the common shares of Carebook not already owned by UIL and its affiliates or associates, at a price of $0.10/Share, for aggregate consideration to the shareholders of Carebook of $4.17M.
  • The Transaction received overwhelming support from the Co.'s shareholders at a special meeting held on Feb. 18, 2025, and the Transaction received final court approval on Feb. 20, 2025.
  • The Co. also intends to submit an application to cease to be a reporting issuer under applicable Canadian securities laws and to otherwise terminate the Co.'s public reporting requirements.

data.title: LifeMD up slightly as it enters behavioral health
data.description:
• LifeMD (NASDAQ:LFMD) is up ~3% in premarket trading Monday after announcing it will start offering behavioral services.
  • The telehealth provider will offer teletherapy, psychiatry and medication management.
  • LifeMD has hired Julian Cohen as SVP, behavioral health & clinical operations. He has more than three decades of experience in the medical and behavioral health industries.

data.title: Carlyle unit NSM Insurance to sell U.S. commercial insurance business
data.description:
• The Carlyle Group (NASDAQ:CG) unit NSM Insurance Group is set to sell its U.S. commercial insurance division to the investment firm New Mountain Capital.
  • The terms of the transaction were not disclosed.
  • New Mountain Capital holds over $55B in AUM.
  • The transaction is expected to close in the next 45 days.
  • NSM's chief commercial lines officer, Aaron Miller, is set to become the CEO of the new entity.

data.title: Meta continues to build AI empire as it expands into Middle East
data.description:
Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) continues to grow its artificial intelligence offerings globally, as the social media giant launched Meta AI today across the Middle East.

Users in Algeria, Egypt, Iraq, Jordan, Libya, Morocco, Saudi Arabia, Tunisia, United Arab Emirates and Yemen can now access Meta AI in Arabic, across Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp and Messenger, Meta announced today.

"With this new chapter in our expansion, millions of users across the region now have unlimited free access to one of the world's most advanced AI assistants conveniently located in the apps you know and love," the company said.

Users in the Middle East and North Africa region will also soon receive multimodal tools, including simultaneous dubbing for Reels, AI image editing and Imagine Me, which is an AI tool that creates images based on user descriptions.

Meta AI reports it is on track to have the world's most widely used AI assistant by the end of 2025. It is currently available in 42 countries and 13 languages.

Meta's open-source approach to AI, is driving higher levels of adoption by developers, according to some analysts.

data.title: Twilio in focus as Morgan Stanley upgrades on improving execution
data.description:
Twilio (NYSE:TWLO) was in the spotlight on Monday as Morgan Stanley upgraded the communications software maker, citing improving execution.

Shares rose 3.5% in premarket trading.

“We are increasingly confident in crediting TWLO's execution toward double-digit growth and operating margin outperformance on back of increased cross-sell, supported by our recent checks,” analyst Meta Marshall wrote in a note to clients. “We see recent sell-off as overdone, providing an attractive entry point, and we upgrade to [Overweight].” Marshall raised her rating on Twilio to Overweight from Equal-Weight and upped her price target to $160 from $144.

Delving a little deeper, Marshall said the selloff in the stock after it reported fourth-quarter results last week was “overdone” and created an “attractive buying opportunity.” Twilio shares now trade at roughly 19 times 2026 free cash flow estimates, well below the peer average of about 24.5 times estimates.

And with recent checks and the exit growth rate from the fourth-quarter showing improvement, Marshall said she has confidence in Twilio's ability to execute the plan it laid out at its analyst day.

“We are encouraged that management continues to run business around double-digit growth in terms of opex investments required, leaving potential for upside to margins if reacceleration we saw exiting 2024 is sustainable,” Marshal wrote.

“Our base case now contemplates achievement of management targets (21-22% operating margins by FY27) a year ahead, driven by revenue upside, Segment efficiencies, moves to lower cost geographies, increasing use of AI internally and product mix shift. On the latter, we continue to expect Segment to achieve operating break-even by 2Q25, and continued traction in sales motions and new features should continue to support profitability upside.”

data.title: FiscalNote divests additional non-core assets for $40M, shares jump
data.description:
Shares of FiscalNote Holdings (NYSE:NOTE) jumped 16% premarket on Monday after the company agreed to divest two portfolio companies within its Global Intelligence business, Oxford Analytica and Dragonfly, to Dow Jones, a division of News Corp (NASDAQ:NWSA), for total consideration of $40M.

The transaction is expected to close in Q1 2025, subject to receipt of antitrust clearance in Austria and other customary closing conditions. Dow Jones's parent company, News Corp (NASDAQ:NWSA), expects to receive a $4M tax benefit in connection with the transaction.

The resulting net proceeds provide FiscalNote a return of capital that helps reduce its senior term loan and strengthens its balance sheet while further simplifying the business as the company continues its ongoing strategic shift to focus on its core Policy platform.

“This divestiture enables us to pay down a significant portion of our senior term loan, resulting in a cumulative paydown of more than 60 percent in the past year. Beyond strengthening our financial position, this move will streamline operations, expand Adjusted EBITDA margins, and accelerate our path to positive free cash flow,” FiscalNote CEO Josh Resnik said.

Oxford Analytica and Dragonfly are each primarily based in the U.K. The businesses have offered their products independently of FiscalNote’s core products.

Oxford Analytica, acquired by FiscalNote in February 2021, produces geopolitical analysis and provides advisory services, while Dragonfly, acquired in January 2023, provides forward-looking global security intelligence and data.

Separately, FiscalNote has engaged John Lee, former head of contracts for Palantir’s U.S. Government Vertical, as a senior strategic advisor to support the implementation of its AI & tech solutions.
